title: Childhood Immunization Visit: Schedule, True vs False Contraindications & Vaccine Hesitancy
subtitle: A healthy 15-month-old is at a well-child visit and is due for vaccines. She has mild rhinorrhea and a low-grade temp, and her parent voices hesitancy. The nurse must screen for true contraindications, vaccinate safely, and counsel the parent.

patient.name: Mia Sandoval
patient.admitDate: Well-child visit. Active, playful, thriving. Mild rhinorrhea and low-grade temp (37.8°C) from a cold. Due for 12–15-month vaccines (MMR, varicella, Hib and PCV boosters, hepatitis A, DTaP catch-up). Parent worried about a 'link between MMR and autism.'

vitals.temp: Mild fussiness, low-grade temp, sore thigh

labs.v: Behind one DTaP — catch-up due today.

nursesNote: Mia Sandoval, 15 months, healthy and thriving at a well-child visit; due for 12–15-month vaccines plus a DTaP catch-up. Mild cold with low-grade temp 37.8°C. Parent voices concern about MMR and autism. No anaphylaxis to prior doses; not immunocompromised. VIS sheets ready; epinephrine available.
providerOrders: Administer due 12–15-month vaccines, including DTaP catch-up
Provide Vaccine Information Statements before administration
Observe 15 minutes post-vaccination for immediate reactions
Anticipatory guidance and routine well-child counseling

steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.sub: What applies to vaccinating this toddler today?

opts.text: A mild cold with low-grade fever is NOT a contraindication — vaccinate today
opts.rat: Minor illness does not delay vaccines.

opts.text: Verify the immunization record and which vaccines are due
Provide the Vaccine Information Statement (VIS) before administering
Confirm there was no anaphylaxis to a prior dose or component
Current antibiotic use for an ear infection means delay all vaccines
opts.rat: False — antibiotic use is not a contraindication.

opts.text: Screen for true contraindications to LIVE vaccines (severe immunocompromise)
Anaphylaxis to a vaccine or component is a true contraindication
Mild illness, antibiotic use, and prematurity are FALSE contraindications
Live vaccines are contraindicated in pregnancy and severe immunosuppression
A family history of vaccine reactions means skip the vaccine
Encephalopathy within 7 days of a prior pertussis dose contraindicates further DTaP
A mild local reaction to a prior dose means stop that series
opts.rat: False — minor local reactions are expected.

steps.title: Prioritize Hypotheses
steps.sub: Order the steps to safely give today's vaccines

opts.text: Review the record, confirm due vaccines, and screen for true contraindications
Provide the VIS and address the parent's questions and hesitancy
Prepare the correct vaccines, doses, sites, and routes
Administer with correct technique and comfort measures
Observe ~15 minutes for an immediate reaction and document
Give multiple due vaccines at the same visit in different sites
Use the vastus lateralis for IM injections in this toddler
Give MMR and varicella subcutaneously
Have epinephrine ready and observe for anaphylaxis
Use comfort measures (positioning, distraction, breastfeeding)
Document lot number, site, route, and VIS date
Delay vaccines because of a mild cold and low-grade fever
opts.rat: Minor illness is not a reason to delay.

opts.text: Withhold a vaccine for current antibiotic use
Routinely give acetaminophen before vaccinating to prevent fever
Skip the 15-minute post-vaccine observation

steps.title: Take Action
steps.sub: Parent says 'I heard MMR causes autism' — sequence your response
steps.inst: Rank the order of actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: Listen and acknowledge the concern without judgment
Provide clear, accurate information (no MMR–autism link; disease risks)
Give a strong, presumptive recommendation and answer questions
Respect the decision; if still declining, document informed refusal
Schedule follow-up and keep the door open to vaccinate later
The nurse vaccinated despite the mild cold and explained why
opts.rat: Correct — minor illness is not a barrier.

opts.text: MMR and varicella were given SQ; others IM in the vastus lateralis
The VIS was provided and the child observed 15 minutes afterward
The autism concern was addressed with accurate information
Lot numbers, sites, and routes were documented
The visit was rescheduled solely because the child had rhinorrhea
All injections were given in one site/leg
The parent was shamed for asking questions
Post-vaccination observation was skipped
